summary: Converged ADR 0341 on the live platform by reconciling a dedicated `open-webui` Keycloak client on `docker-runtime-lv3`, switching Open WebUI to repo-managed OIDC for routine sign-in, keeping the local bootstrap admin as break-glass recovery, and aligning the stale Keycloak playbook and topology surfaces with the already-live Docker runtime placement.

verification.observed: The targeted pytest slice covering the Keycloak playbook, Open WebUI playbook, Open WebUI runtime, Docker runtime, and PostgreSQL runtime returned `67 passed in 28.33s`; `make syntax-check-open-webui` and `make syntax-check-keycloak` also passed before the live replay.

`make converge-open-webui` completed successfully on 2026-04-03 after the topology and OIDC discovery fixes, with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 ok=273 changed=3 failed=0` and `proxmox-host ok=40 changed=4 failed=0`.

verification.check: Public Keycloak discovery and Open WebUI OIDC redirect
verification.observed: A direct fetch of `https://sso.example.com/realms/lv3/.well-known/openid-configuration` returned `HTTP 200` with issuer `https://sso.example.com/realms/lv3`, and `http://100.64.0.1:8008/oauth/oidc/login` returned `HTTP 302` to the Keycloak `open-webui` authorization endpoint.

The mirrored bootstrap admin from `/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/open-webui/admin-password.txt` authenticated successfully at `http://100.64.0.1:8008/api/v1/auths/signin` as `ops@example.com` with role `admin`, and the same credentials also succeeded directly against the runtime on `http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/v1/auths/signin`.

notes: Open WebUI now uses the internal Keycloak discovery URL derived from `platform_service_topology.keycloak.urls.internal` because the container could not reliably hairpin through the public `sso.example.com` route even after the edge `502` was repaired.

The downstream Keycloak client reconcile path depends on the controller-local bootstrap admin mirror at `/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/keycloak/bootstrap-admin-password.txt`; that mirror was resynced from `/etc/lv3/keycloak/bootstrap-admin-password` during the correction loop before the final successful replay.

correction_loop.surface: Keycloak client reconciliation, shared SSO edge health, and Open WebUI runtime OIDC discovery
correction_loop.repair_action: Aligned the top-level Keycloak playbook and topology to the actual live host `docker-runtime-lv3`, repaired the public Keycloak edge publication with a narrow `nginx-lv3` replay when `sso.example.com` returned `HTTP 502`, resynced the controller-local Keycloak bootstrap admin password mirror from the runtime after recovery, and changed Open WebUI's OIDC provider URL to the internal Keycloak discovery endpoint reachable from inside the container.
correction_loop.verification: The final replay completed without failures, `sso.example.com` discovery returned `HTTP 200`, `http://100.64.0.1:8008/oauth/oidc/login` now redirects into Keycloak, and the break-glass login still returns the expected `ops@example.com` admin identity.
